description_long: This is a metal-framed bunk bed with two stacked sleeping platforms, each fitted with a thin, worn-looking mattress. The frame is constructed from tubular metal bars, showing signs of rust and age, especially at the joints and along the rails. Both the head and foot of the bed feature vertical slats, providing structural support and a utilitarian aesthetic. A simple, narrow ladder is attached to one side, allowing access to the upper bunk. The mattresses are uncovered, exposing a textured, slightly stained surface that suggests heavy use. The overall appearance is industrial and functional, suitable for institutional or urban environments such as shelters, hostels, or dormitories.
description: A rusted metal bunk bed with two bare, worn mattresses and a simple ladder, featuring vertical slat headboards and footboards. The design is utilitarian and industrial, showing signs of age and heavy use.
description_view_0: Front view showing both bunks, the full width of the bed, and the vertical slat design of the head and footboards.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the bed, the thinness of the mattresses, and the tubular metal frame.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the attached ladder for upper bunk access and the slatted footboard.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other end of the bed, with the same slatted design and visible mattress surfaces.
